title: Turned to Core
wikitext:
[]: [SPOILERS] Turned to Core             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Mon May 20, 2013 6:35 pm Reichu has brought up a certain translation from the script a few times now about how when Shinji looks out on the post 3I landscape he see everything has "turned to core". This seems a very odd description, as it appears at first glance that the planet is simply covered in red dust, or "LCL fallout" as I've been dubbing it. However, the idea of this substance being "core" material is actually a lot closer to reality, as evidenced by a subtle visual cue which can be seen in the "fallout". Firstly, to get it out of the way: Core's in Evangelion are big red spheres, but moreover Cores in NTE have subtle speckled patterns, moving viscously just below their surfaces. This is a very subtle effect, but does appear to be present on all cores in the new films. You'll need to click the images to really see the "speckles". Core Speckles  SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Mon May 20, 2013 6:45 pm It's... MOVING. Just when you thought the post-TI-landscape couldn't get any creepier... Note, however, that the texture of the ground doesn't seem to be crystaline (like your usual core) but more like it's caked with some softer material, kinda like dried sand or blood (they leave footprints)

[]:             Quote Post by BlueBasilisk » Mon May 20, 2013 6:55 pm Kendrix wrote: It's... MOVING. Just when you thought the post-TI-landscape couldn't get any creepier... Note, however, that the texture of the ground doesn't seem to be crystaline (like your usual core) but more like it's caked with some softer material, kinda like dried sand or blood (they leave footprints)

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Mon May 20, 2013 6:56 pm I'd find it more likely that all living things liquified and then coalesced into a crust of this material, and possibly the FoI.

[]:             Quote Post by Shinoyami65 » Mon May 20, 2013 6:58 pm Wow, that's...really something. That really is a core....Has the entire planet become a big, spherical core for a larger entity? Not sure where that dust came from but conceivably it could be made from the ruins of other structures or just the remains of the ground from before it turned into a core.

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Mon May 20, 2013 6:59 pm It's probably ground up core gravel/particles/dust of all different sizes. As already mentioned some very violent events have taken place in the Tokyo-3 area and as the Door of Guf closes and the anti-gravity magic is gone all that stuff comes crashign down and amongst it are everything from FoIs to buildings, boulders, etc. The corefied material seems to be clearly susceptible to good old erosion.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Mon May 20, 2013 7:00 pm As a follow up post to the last one, it turns out that this moving speckled pattern is by no means exclusive to the "fallout" dust coating Tokyo-3. In fact, the effect appears to be omnipresent among the Failures of Infinity as well. I'm unable to make animations of the following, but hopefully you can make out the patterns. Core Speckles FoIs  SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Stillborn » Mon May 20, 2013 7:07 pm Maybe irrevelant, but it strongly reminds me of Magatsuchi from Nocturne. Red, speckled "fluid" that supernatural beings, like demons, angels and even gods, prey upon. It can be extracted from humans cause it's composed from strong emotions. Easiest way is through suffering. http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=bXTgz6m6HD4 There are levels of Hell that are build from this.

[]:             Quote Post by Shinoyami65 » Mon May 20, 2013 7:14 pm There are cores everywhere...Eva-01 seems to have become core-ified as well, despite being intact at the end of 2.0. So whatever happened in the timeskip affected Eva-01 as well, it wasn't immune despite the power it obtained at the end of 2.0. Possibly that could indicate that Eva-01 wasn't responsible for starting whatever happened during the timeskip. The "core grid" around the Moon is rather mystifying, though. Possibly the Moon was going to be converted into a core, but the process was aborted? Some things seem to be completely core, while others seem to be "coated in core"...and Eva-01 itself still seems to be converting other objects into cores.

[]:             Quote Post by BlueBasilisk » Mon May 20, 2013 7:16 pm Stillborn wrote: Maybe irrevelant, but it strongly reminds me of Magatsuchi from Nocturne. Red, speckled "fluid" that supernatural beings, like demons, angels and even gods, prey upon. It can be extracted from humans cause it's composed from strong emotions. Easiest way is through suffering. http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=bXTgz6m6HD4 There are levels of Hell that are build from this.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Mon May 20, 2013 7:20 pm Beats me what the hell is going on. The moon being so low and spinning so fast defies physics - it makes me think of a Gurren-Lagann style switch over with an artificial moon. I know it's not good sense to bring in an element of the old series, the speckle effect reminds me of the starfield effect that represents the resident souls of Eva-01 and Eva-00. And don't forget the little red dots from EoE. It's as though the cores have hundreds of little soul fragments floating inside, with the brighter area of red indicating the soul itself. The way I see it, it's all alive.

[]:             Quote Post by Stillborn » Mon May 20, 2013 7:32 pm BlueBasilisk wrote: Gendo is actually Hikawa and trying to cause the Conception! :O

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon May 20, 2013 7:51 pm Shinoyami65 wrote: Eva-01 seems to have become core-ified as well

[]:             Quote Post by Shinoyami65 » Mon May 20, 2013 8:19 pm Reichu wrote: At most, we can say that the inside of the tesseract is filled with this core material, and Eva-01 only looks red due to being suspended in the stuff. [wkimg width=400]Eva3-33 C0090 eva01-eye.jpg[/wkimg] There's some notes from Anno in 1.0 CRC about the glistening core effect. Let me translate that...

[]:             Quote Post by K40s » Mon May 20, 2013 8:48 pm there's the scene when eva-02 is force inserting the igniter into Wunder's main engine, Eva-02 is seen ejecting her mechanical arm that turns into core and blows up.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Mon May 20, 2013 9:43 pm I realized this upon first viewing. I thought everyone else already knew and I was just late to the part, what with everyone else having already seen the camrip and everything. It has been established since 1.11 that the cores had these movies speckles in it. Watch the scene where Unit 01 pounds at the 4th Angel's core with its own broken rib. You see the specks in the core ripple. Makes me wonder what SEELE had planned, and if this aspect is also a part of Gendo's plan.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Mon May 20, 2013 10:28 pm Lest I forget. Marl.06 Visor lights  SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Mon May 20, 2013 11:15 pm I was assuming that the stuff encasing Unit-01 and filling up the inverted pyramid was Bakelite....

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Tue May 21, 2013 3:06 am FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: Makes me wonder what SEELE had planned, and if this aspect is also a part of Gendo's plan.

[]:             Quote Post by Giji Shinka » Tue May 21, 2013 4:37 am I noticed that the door of Guf has those sparkly core things too.
